title: eriogonum angulosum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum angulosum subsp. typicum, also known as the alpine buckwheat or buckwheat sage, is a perennial wildflower species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, derived from its saponin, flavonoid, and phenolic content. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, digestive improvement, and immune system support, making it a potential treatment for various conditions. The herbal preparations of Eriogonum angulosum subsp. typicum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
